questions.text: I hold the view that my needs can always wait if someone else's are more pressing.
I would describe my influence style as collaborative.
I tend not to notice when someone is putting on a brave face.
In high-pressure situations, I look to collaborate and distribute the load.
I complete tasks well before their deadlines whenever possible.
I find that committing too firmly to plans can make me miss out on better alternatives.
I find that I am more interested in the factual accuracy of what is said than in how it was received.
I detect when someone is subtly seeking validation in the way they tell a story.
I find that the felt weight of another's suffering has a clarifying effect on my priorities that nothing else can replicate.
I believe a project needs one clear leader to succeed.
I think that analyzing emotionally difficult situations through a lens of detached reasoning is the method that produces the most impartial and well-balanced conclusions.
My emotional equilibrium is rarely disrupted for long.
I find deep satisfaction in achieving goals I set for myself.
I believe that the separation of emotional content from decision-making content is the hallmark of clear thinking under pressure.
I find that my emotional responses are measured and proportional to the situation.
I feel that fully engaging with the present is the only legacy that truly matters.
I notice that my emotional highs are exhilarating but my lows are equally deep.
I routinely invest in education and training that advances my own skills.
